features.summary: Costco reported strong Q2 2025 results with net income of $1.788 billion ($4.02 per share) versus $1.743 billion ($3.92 per share) in Q2 2024. Net sales increased 9.1% to $62.53 billion, driven by comparable sales growth of 6.8% (9.1% adjusted for gas deflation and FX). US comp sales grew 8.3%, while international markets showed strong performance with Canada up 4.6% and other international up 1.7% (both significantly higher when adjusted for FX). E-commerce continued robust growth at 20.9% comp sales increase. The company opened minimal new locations in Q2 but plans 28 new openings for fiscal 2025.
features.management_outlook: Management projects 28 new warehouse openings in fiscal 2025 with strong international expansion plans, targeting just over half of future growth in the US and just under half internationally. The company implemented a new employee agreement with $1 hourly wage increases and enhanced benefits, reflecting continued investment in workforce. Management expressed confidence in navigating potential tariff impacts through global buying power, supplier relationships, and merchandising flexibility. They emphasized focus on delivering value to members while maintaining operational efficiency and highlighted opportunities in retail media and personalized digital communications.
features.risks: Foreign exchange headwinds are expected to continue impacting international results, with FX negatively affecting net income by $57 million in Q2. Potential tariff implementation creates uncertainty, particularly given that about one-third of US sales are imported goods. The new employee agreement will create a 13 basis point SG&A headwind starting in March, with mid-single digit net year-over-year impact. Inflation returned at low single digits during the quarter, with fresh foods showing the highest inflation, particularly in meat and bakery categories driven by egg price increases.
features.key_drivers: Traffic growth of 5.7% worldwide demonstrates strong member engagement and frequency increases. Non-foods category led growth with mid-teens comparable sales, driven by successful treasure hunt merchandising and premium electronics. Kirkland Signature private label continues outpacing overall business growth with successful new product launches and international sourcing improvements. Membership fee increases are flowing through with deferred accounting benefits expected over the next four quarters. E-commerce strength was broad-based across categories, with Costco Logistics achieving record holiday performance and continued expansion of the curated marketplace platform.
